ogTitle: Loomberah vs Lord Howe Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Loomberah and Lord Howe Island.
heading: Loomberah vs Lord Howe Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Loomberah, New South Wales and Lord Howe Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lord Howe Island has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Loomberah (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Loomberah or Lord Howe Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Loomberah has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Lord Howe Island scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Loomberah scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Lord Howe Island scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Loomberah or Lord Howe Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lord Howe Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
